design_name: Compound gear set with two spur gears of different sizes mounted on a shared cylindrical hub, centered at the origin in XY, standing upright along the Z axis, with a central bore through the full hub length.
prompt: A compound gear set (two gears of different sizes on a shared hub), standing upright along the Z axis, centered at the origin in XY. The cylindrical hub is 16mm in diameter and 40mm long (Z=0 to Z=40). A large spur gear with 24 teeth (star-polygon profile: 54mm tip diameter (27mm radius), 44mm root diameter (22mm radius)) is located from Z=3 to Z=13 (10mm face width). A smaller spur gear with 14 teeth (star-polygon profile: 32mm tip diameter (16mm radius), 26mm root diameter (13mm radius)) is located from Z=20 to Z=30 (10mm face width). An 8mm diameter bore runs through the full hub length along Z.
primitive_plan_summary: Compound gear set with two spur gears of different sizes mounted on a shared cylindrical hub, centered at the origin in XY, standing upright along the Z axis, with a central bore through the full hub length.

verifier_score.feedback: All constraints met. The model correctly implements all requested features: (1) Cylindrical hub: 16mm diameter, 40mm long from Z=0 to Z=40 — confirmed by bounding box zlen=40, zmin=0, zmax=40. (2) Large spur gear with 24 teeth: star-polygon profile with 54mm tip diameter (27mm radius) and 44mm root diameter (22mm radius), located Z=3 to Z=13 — confirmed by bounding box xlen=ylen=54mm matching the tip diameter. Visually the large gear is clearly visible at the bottom with the correct number of teeth. (3) Small spur gear with 14 teeth: star-polygon profile with 32mm tip diameter (16mm radius) and 26mm root diameter (13mm radius), located Z=20 to Z=30 — visible in the rendered views as the intermediate-sized gear above the large one. (4) 8mm diameter bore through full hub length — clearly visible in the center/top-down view showing the through-hole. The part is valid, watertight, has no self-intersections, and the volume (~30,230 mm³) is physically plausible for the described geometry. The rendered images confirm two distinct gears of different sizes on a shared hub with a central bore, all consistent with the prompt specifications.
